note: High-speed lifts to the accessible observatory; the mall base and entrances are fully step-free with accessible toilets. Easy and modern.
Vast collection in a fully accessible building with lifts, step-free galleries, loaner wheelchairs and shuttle access; flat once inside. A little outside the center.
The grand plaza is flat and step-free to cross, and lifts reach the main hall (the famous frontal staircase has an accessible alternative); the surrounding gardens are largely flat.
A huge, flat but intensely crowded night market with tight lanes and a partly basement food court reached by stairs/lift; navigable slowly but a real squeeze at peak. Go early.
A beautiful historic temple with a threshold step at entrances and dense incense-filled courtyards; partly accessible with a small step, very crowded at prayer times.
A large flat central park with wide paved step-free paths, accessible toilets and shade; excellent for strollers, wheelchairs and a relaxed senior stroll.
The thermal valley area has some accessible public facilities and a flat main path, but many private baths and the terraced hillside involve steps; the accessible library and park are easy. Mixed.
The gondola cabins have step-free/accessible boarding at the modern stations, offering tea-hill views; some hilltop tea houses beyond are steep. The ride itself is accessible.
A flat pedestrianized shopping-and-street-food district with step-free streets; crowded but easy to wheel and stroll through.
A famous flat single-street night market that is step-free but extremely crowded and tight; manageable slowly off-peak. The adjacent temple has a step.
The classic Taipei 101 viewpoint is reached by a steep staircase hike with no accessible route; not suitable for wheels. Alt: the Taipei 101 observatory for the reverse view.
